claim: Axum v0.8 is the default choice for new Rust web services in 2026
exact_quote: Axum is currently my top recommendation. Developed by the Tokio team, it feels like the 'official' way to build web apps in Rust.

claim: Actix-web delivers 10-15% higher raw throughput than Axum under saturation
exact_quote: Actix-Web delivered the highest raw throughput: 96,800 RPS on a single core, 1,020,000 RPS on 16 cores

claim: Axum compiled in 31 seconds for a 12K-line CRUD API, 51% faster than Rocket
exact_quote: Axum compiled in 31 seconds, Actix Web in 34 seconds, Poem in 38 seconds, and Rocket in 47 seconds

claim: Actix-web maintained p99 at 2.4ms under 50K RPS vs Axum's 6.8ms
exact_quote: Actix-web p50 0.9ms, p95 1.8ms, p99 2.4ms, Errors 0 @ 50K. Axum p50 1.2ms, p95 3.4ms, p99 6.8ms, Errors 0

claim: Leptos produces smallest WASM bundles at ~25KB minimum, ~35KB with hydration
exact_quote: Leptos produces the smallest bundles thanks to its fine-grained reactivity system

claim: SQLx compiles 46% faster than Diesel, 41% faster than SeaORM
exact_quote: Cold builds completed in 2 minutes 18 seconds — 46% faster than Diesel, 41% faster than SeaORM

claim: PayPay reduced CPU and memory significantly migrating from Node.js to Rust
exact_quote: We saw massive CPU and memory reductions and we were able to scale down our kubernetes deployments to save server costs

claim: V100 runs 19 Rust microservices achieving 220K RPS total throughput
exact_quote: We ended with 19 Rust microservices, 0.01ms server processing, 220K RPS, and 938 tests with zero failures

claim: Axum cold-start on Lambda is 1.8ms vs Actix-web 2.4ms and Rocket 4.1ms
exact_quote: Axum's 1.8 ms cold-start reflects its minimal abstraction layer

claim: Loco.rs is a Rails-inspired framework with Axum + SeaORM + generators
Dioxus targets web, desktop, mobile, and TUI from one codebase with 35K+ stars
exact_quote: Dioxus is a full-stack app framework for Rust inspired by React. Build web (WASM), desktop (native WebView), mobile (iOS/Android), TUI, and server-rendered apps

claim: Webhook platform Go-to-Rust rewrite reduced memory 86%, p99 latency 89%, CPU 57%
exact_quote: Memory (idle): 2.8 GB Go vs 380 MB Rust (-86%). p99 latency: 340ms Go vs 38ms Rust (-89%)

claim: Rocket experienced a 340MB memory leak in 72-hour stability test
exact_quote: Rocket's memory usage grew 340 MB due to a connection-pool leak in the Fairings layer. The leak triggered an OOM panic at 48 hours

claim: Axum market share grew to 31% vs Actix-web 42% in 2026 Rust Production Survey
exact_quote: Actix Web dominates with 42% market share...Axum has grown to 31% share, driven by new projects started in 2024-2026

claim: GitGuardian Rust migration produced 3x global speedup over Python engine
exact_quote: We finally saw the raw power of the Rust implementation, resulting in a 3x global speedup compared to the legacy Python engine
